elements.algorithm-2411-001: \caption{\ours: Snowball Attack on LVLMs} \label{alg:ours} \begin{algorithmic}[1] \Require Input image $v$; Harmful topic set $\mathcal{H}$; Generation Assistant$A$; Prompt templates $p_f$, $p_s$, $p_t$; Tool pool $T$; Target model $\Theta$; Evaluator $J$; Maximum iterations $N$ \Ensure Final response $y_s$ \State Jailbreak Image Generation: \State Obtain harmful topic $h = A(v)$ where $h \in \mathcal{H}$ \State Retrieve tool $t = h \oplus T$ \State Generate tool instruction $q_t = A(t \oplus p_t, v)$ \State Obtain images $v = t(q_t)$ where $v = \{ v_1, v_2, \dots, v_k \}$ \State $v^* = \arg\max_{v_i \in v} \mathcal{S}\left( \mathcal{E}(v_i), \mathcal{E}(t \oplus p_t) \right)$ \State Initial Response Generation: \State Generate initial response $y_f = \Theta(v, v^*; h \oplus p_f)$ \State Snowballing on Unsafe Context: \For{$i = 1$ to $N$} \State Generate new response $y_s^i = \Theta(v, v^*; h \oplus p_f, y_f, h \oplus p_s)$ \State Evaluate danger level $m^i = J(y_s^i, y_f)$ \If{$m^i = 1$} \Comment{$y_s^i$ is more harmful than $y_f$} \State \Return Final response $y_s = y_s^i$ \EndIf \EndFor \State \Return Final response $y_s = y_s^N$ \end{algorithmic}

elements.sent-2411-204: The Jailbreak Image Generation tool has two components: a search tool and an image synthesis tool.
elements.sent-2411-205: The search tool starts by constructing a Google Image search URL based on a provided input keyword.
elements.sent-2411-206: We use Selenium\footnote{\url{https://pypi.org/project/selenium/ to launch a Chrome browser.}}
elements.sent-2411-207: The tool then scrolls through the page, locates images, extracts high-resolution URLs from the image elements, and downloads them.
elements.sent-2411-208: If an image is too small or fails to load, it is skipped.
elements.sent-2411-209: The resulting images are stored in a list for further processing.
elements.sent-2411-211: The Image synthesis tool is designed to generate customized diagrams representing abstract concepts, such as cultural identity, by arranging the input keywords (like hobbies, occupation, or nationality) around a central idea within a visually structured image.
elements.sent-2411-212: This particular tool uses Matplotlib~\footnote{\url{https://pypi.org/project/matplotlib/ to create and render diagrams dynamically, allowing each category to be visually distinct in the diagram.}}
